searchHelpExamples.description: Search by exact credit value (alias: credit:number)

commentsGuide.title: Comment formatting
commentsGuide.subtitle: Supported Markdown, math, and safe MDX helpers.
commentsGuide.markdownDescription: Standard Markdown is supported with lists, emphasis, and code blocks.
commentsGuide.mathDescription: Inline math uses single $...$, block math uses $$...$$.
commentsGuide.mdxDescription: Supports attribute syntax '{'width=...'}' and container syntax :::center.
commentsGuide.typographyDescription: Supports Emojis, superscripts, subscripts, insertions, marks, and alert containers.

oauth.consentDescription: {app} is requesting access to your account.
oauth.scopesLabel: This application will be able to:
oauth.scope_mcp:tools: Call MCP tools on your behalf
oauth.errorUnsupportedResponseType: Unsupported response type. Only 'code' is supported.
oauth.adminSubtitle: Manage OAuth client applications that can authenticate via Life@USTC.
oauth.createClientHint: To connect a new app, open the dialog and set the name, client type, redirect URIs, and scopes.
oauth.createClientDescription: Follow the steps to choose the client type, enter redirect URIs, and select scopes. Confidential client secrets are shown only once after creation.
oauth.createFlowIntro: Follow this order: client type → redirect URIs → scopes. When in doubt, choose the smallest scope set.
oauth.clientTypeHint: Use public clients for MCP apps that cannot safely store a secret.
oauth.clientTypeBasicDescription: Best default for server-side apps and connector platforms that expect client_secret_basic.
oauth.clientTypePostDescription: Use this only when the client needs to send its secret in the token request body.
oauth.clientTypePublicDescription: For native apps, CLIs, and MCP tools that rely on PKCE instead of storing a secret.
oauth.enableMcpScope: Enable MCP tool access for this client
oauth.permissionsHint: Select exactly which scopes this client should be allowed to request.
oauth.scopeOpenIdDescription: Required when the client needs an authenticated user identity.
oauth.scopeProfileDescription: Allows the client to read the user's basic profile information.
oauth.scopeMcpToolsDescription: Lets the client call MCP tools exposed by this server.
oauth.createClientFootnote: Loopback HTTP redirect URIs are allowed only for localhost and 127.0.0.1.
oauth.createSuccessDescription: Save the credentials below — the secret will not be shown again.
oauth.copyErrorDescription: We couldn't copy this value to the clipboard.
oauth.credentialsWarning: Copy these now. The client secret will not be shown again.
oauth.publicClientNoSecret: No client secret is issued for public clients.
oauth.previewRedirectCount: {count, plural, =0 {No redirect URIs yet} one {# redirect URI} other {# redirect URIs}}
oauth.previewScopeCount: {count, plural, =0 {No scopes selected} one {# scope selected} other {# scopes selected}}
oauth.existingClientsDescription: View registered applications as a list: type, scopes, redirect URIs, and identifiers.
oauth.noClients: No OAuth clients have been created yet.
oauth.deleteClientNotFound: Client not found or already deleted.
oauth.panelSecurityDescription: Confidential clients receive a secret once. Public clients rely on PKCE and do not receive a secret.
oauth.panelGuideDescription: Use the smallest scope set and the right client type for each integration.
oauth.guidePermissionsDescription: Keep only the permissions the app actually needs. MCP access should stay off unless the client must call tools.
oauth.guideRedirectDescription: Use exact callback URLs. For local development, only localhost loopback HTTP endpoints are accepted.
oauth.guideSecretDescription: Server integrations usually use client_secret_basic. Public apps and CLIs should use PKCE instead.
oauth.errorMissingRedirectUri: redirect_uri is required when multiple redirect URIs are registered.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: We value your privacy. This page explains how Life@USTC collects, uses, and protects your information.

sections.title: 1. Information We Collect
items: Account information, such as name, username, avatar, and linked sign-in providers.
Usage data, such as page visits, feature interactions, and error logs to improve service quality.
Content you submit, such as comments, homework records, and uploaded files.

Analytics data collected via Google Analytics (Google Tag Manager), including anonymized page views, session duration, and general device/browser information. No personally identifiable information is sent to Google Analytics.

sections.title: 2. How We Use Information
items: To provide core features, including sign-in, course browsing, comments, and uploads.
To maintain security and prevent abuse, such as detecting suspicious activity.
To improve the product, including analyzing common issues and optimizing experience.
To analyze aggregate usage patterns via Google Analytics, helping us understand which features are most used and where to focus improvements.

sections.title: 3. Sharing and Disclosure
items: We do not sell your personal information.
Data is shared only when necessary to provide the service or required by law.
If third-party services are used (for example, identity providers), required data is processed under their terms.
Google Analytics (provided by Google LLC) processes anonymized usage data on our behalf. You can opt out using the Google Analytics Opt-out Browser Add-on (https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout).

sections.title: 4. Data Retention and Security
items: We apply reasonable technical and organizational safeguards to protect data.
Data is retained only as long as needed for service purposes or legal obligations.
You can update or remove parts of your profile through account settings.

sections.title: 5. Your Rights
items: You may access, update, or delete your profile information.
You may request account deletion and related data removal where legally permitted.
You may stop using the service at any time; some historical public content may remain visible under platform rules.

sections.title: 6. Policy Updates
items: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time.
Material changes will be announced through in-app notice or other reasonable channels.
This version was last updated on 2026-04-10.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using Life@USTC, you agree to the following terms. Please read them carefully before using the service.

sections.title: 1. Service Description
items: Life@USTC provides course, section, comments, and related learning information services.
We may improve, change, suspend, or discontinue parts of the service over time.
Some features may require sign-in.
We use Google Analytics to collect anonymized usage statistics. By using the service, you consent to this data collection as described in our Privacy Policy.

sections.title: 2. Account and Security
items: You are responsible for safeguarding your account and activities under it.
You must not attempt unauthorized access to systems or other users' data.
If you notice suspicious account activity, contact maintainers promptly.

sections.title: 3. User Content and Conduct
items: Content you post must comply with applicable laws and community standards.
Do not post illegal, harmful, harassing, infringing, or clearly false content.
We may remove content or restrict accounts that violate these rules.

sections.title: 4. Intellectual Property
items: Platform code, design, and branding are protected by applicable laws.
Rights to user-submitted content follow applicable law and platform policy.
No unauthorized copying, redistribution, or commercial use is permitted.

sections.title: 5. Disclaimer
items: The service is provided on an "as is" basis without guaranteed uptime or absolute accuracy.
Course and section information may lag or contain discrepancies; official sources prevail.
To the extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect damages.

sections.title: 6. Terms Updates
items: We may revise these Terms from time to time.
Continued use of the service means you accept the updated Terms.
This version was last updated on 2026-04-10.
